document, but changing it is not allowed.

[This is the first released version of the Lesser GPL.  It also counts as the
successor of the GNU Library Public License, version 2, hence the version
number 2.1.]

Full text: https://www.gnu.org/licenses/old-licenses/lgpl-2.1.html

---

FFmpeg bundled with All Audio

Official FFmpeg project website:

https://ffmpeg.org/

These binaries are provided separately from the All Audio application source code,
which is licensed under the MIT License. See the repository LICENSE file.

## Windows and Linux binaries

LGPL static builds from BtbN/FFmpeg-Builds:

https://github.com/BtbN/FFmpeg-Builds

Artifacts used:

- `ffmpeg-master-latest-win64-lgpl.zip`
- `ffmpeg-master-latest-linux64-lgpl.tar.xz`

## macOS binaries

Static builds from the eugeneware/ffmpeg-static project (release `b6.1.1`):

https://github.com/eugeneware/ffmpeg-static

Upstream sources for macOS builds are documented in that repository (Intel builds
from evermeet.cx, Apple Silicon builds from osxexperts.net). These static builds
may include GPL-licensed components. See FFmpeg-NOTICES.md for details.

To obtain FFmpeg source code, refer to:

https://ffmpeg.org/download.html
